problem: A function $f$ is defined for all real numbers and satisfies $f(2+x)=f(2-x)$ and $f(7+x)=f(7-x)$ for all $x.$ If $f(0) = 0,$ what is the least number of roots $f(x)=0$ must have in the interval $-1000\leq x \leq 1000$?

classified_questions.question: Can you describe what the first equation $f(2+x)=f(2-x)$ means in terms of the function $f$?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: What does the second equation $f(7+x)=f(7-x)$ tell you about the function $f$?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: How can we use the given equations to infer the periodicity of the function $f$?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Based on the conditions on $f(2+x)$ and $f(2-x)$, can you determine any specific properties of the function $f$?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: Given that $f(0) = 0$, what can you conclude about the roots of the function $f$ in the interval $-1000\leq x \leq 1000$?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Can you provide an example of a function that satisfies all the given conditions and has the minimum number of roots as calculated before?
classified_questions.label: b
